danger_signals.visual_form: System designation/classification in star map interface
danger_signals.meaning: High danger zones with frequent pirate activity and minimal law enforcement

danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile ship targeting/weapon fire from authority vessels
danger_signals.meaning: Player has violated trade laws or failed to pay fines; imminent combat threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Visible energy signatures at jump entry/exit points lasting hours
danger_signals.meaning: Trackable location marker indicating pirate/assassin interception opportunity

danger_signals.visual_form: Game notification of illegal goods (narcotics, nerve gas, weapons, slaves)
danger_signals.meaning: Contraband in hold creates fine risk or combat escalation with authorities

danger_signals.visual_form: Combat-engaged hostile vessels engaged in laser fire exchanges
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring evasive maneuvers and defensive/offensive response

safety_signals.meaning: Established, civilized systems with strong government presence and minimal piracy
Safe zone with law enforcement presence and trading infrastructure

safety_signals.visual_form: Cargo manifest showing food, computer parts, and non-contraband items
safety_signals.meaning: No police interaction risk; unrestricted trade allowed in all systems

safety_signals.visual_form: Automated navigation system control to destination
safety_signals.meaning: Safe passage through known routes to designated planet/station without manual piloting threat

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Federation military ships identified in combat encounters
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Based in Sol system; presence in Federation-controlled or allied star systems

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Empire military ships identified in combat encounters
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Based in Achenar system; presence in Empire-controlled or allied star systems

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Non-faction hostiles in lawless space; unmarked hostile vessels
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Concentrated in anarchic systems like Riedquat and Phekda; sparse law enforcement presence

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Authority vessels that escalate to hostile status upon law violations
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Present in law-governed systems; appear at docking checkpoints and patrol routes

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Cold War political tension (Federation vs Empire cease-fire)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Two competing faction bases in Sol and Achenar systems; player can align with either independently
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Military missions available from both factions; conflicting objectives create strategic tension in player career path

world_state_to_visual.world_state: System governance variations (law vs anarchy spectrum)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Star map classification distinguishes Core worlds from anarchic systems
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Core worlds exhibit organized commerce and police presence; anarchic systems feature frequent combat encounters and piracy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Commodity price fluctuations visible in trade interface; specific high-profit routes identified (Barnard's Star-Sol)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player economic strategy determined by system accessibility and goods legality; black market only active in certain jurisdictions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cargo manifest flags illegal items (narcotics, nerve gas, weapons, slaves) by system jurisdiction
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Police enforcement trigger varies by system; high-profit illegal goods carry escalating fine/combat risk

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Combat rating system displays (Harmless → Elite) based on destroyed enemy count
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPC enemy behavior may adjust based on player reputation; higher ratings suggest increased difficulty

environmental_storytelling.example: Core worlds vs anarchic systems (Riedquat, Phekda) communicate civilization level and danger without explicit narrative

environmental_storytelling.technique: Hyperspace cloud remnants as environmental evidence
environmental_storytelling.example: Visible energy signatures at jump points indicate prior traffic and create tactical vulnerability for ambush
Slavery and narcotics illegal in some systems but valuable black market elsewhere; reveals regional moral/political frameworks

environmental_storytelling.technique: Faction base locations as geopolitical markers
environmental_storytelling.example: Federation in Sol, Empire in Achenar; establishes known power centers and implied sphere-of-influence boundaries

environmental_storytelling.technique: Police checkpoint enforcement as social order manifestation
environmental_storytelling.example: Legitimate ownership verification via manual codes communicates governance paranoia and bureaucratic control

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Routine spot-checks for legitimate ownership; non-hostile verification encounters

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Player carrying illegal goods or unpaid fines
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Escalation to hostile engagement; attack initiation upon detection

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Encounter in anarchic or remote systems
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Combat engagement with laser fire exchange; slingshot attack patterns in realistic physics framework
Automated docking availability; safe passage in controlled airspace
Mission briefing and combat deployment; potential rank promotion on success
Economic behavior implicit in price fluctuation; establish profitable/loss trade routes

notes: Frontier: Elite II uses systemic world-state communication rather than explicit visual design. The star map classification (Core vs anarchic) is the primary danger signal; government structure directly determines encounter probability. Faction identity lacks explicit visual sigils in available documentation but is communicated through system location (Sol/Achenar bases) and military mission availability. Political tension (Federation-Empire cease-fire) is narratively established but mechanically manifested through independent parallel rank systems. Hyperspace cloud mechanics create a tension system where player traversal becomes visible to threats. The economy itself (commodity legality variations) is a form of political/cultural world-state communication. Newtonian physics simulation reinforces the 'hard sci-fi' tone and danger through momentum-based vulnerability. No color-coding or explicit visual faction markers documented.
